sections.name: Lady's Whisper

link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 49
link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 49

sections.text: *Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this NPC.*

link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 49
link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 49

traits.name: Unique
traits.text: A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.

traits.name: Evil
traits.text: Evil effects often manipulate energy from evil-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to good divine servants or divine servants of good deities. A creature with this trait is evil in alignment.

traits.name: Undead
traits.text: Once living, these creatures were infused after death with negative energy and soul-corrupting evil magic. When reduced to 0 Hit Points, an undead creature is destroyed. Undead creatures are damaged by positive energy, are healed by negative energy, and don't benefit from healing effects.

automatic_abilities.name: Negative Recovery
automatic_abilities.text: Lady's Whisper can choose whether or not it takes positive damage.

automatic_abilities.name: Sibilant Whispers
automatic_abilities.text: Each creature that begins its turn within the aura and can hear the ominous whispers must succeed at a DC 30 Will save or become frightened 2. On a success, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can't hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item or a creature with a strong alignment.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text: This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.

traits.name: Emotion
traits.text: This effect alters a creature's emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

traits.name: Enchantment
traits.text: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the enchantment school of magic, typically involving mind control, emotion alteration, and other mental effects.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target's mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

universal_monster_ability.name: Negative Healing
universal_monster_ability.text: A creature with negative healing draws health from negative energy rather than positive energy. It is damaged by positive damage and is not healed by positive healing effects. It does not take negative damage, and it is healed by negative effects that heal undead.

modifiers.name: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

interaction_abilities.name: Death's Grace
interaction_abilities.text: Lady's Whisper can choose whether or not it counts as undead for effects that affect undead differently. Even if it does not count as undead, Lady's Whisper never counts as a living creature.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still use your Strength modifier when calculating damage.

ability.name: Gatekeeper's Will
ability.text: Lady's Whisper mentally causes any doors or portals within the Gate of Nhimbaloth (areas **B60** to **B64** and areas **C1** through **C18**) to open or shut. If Lady's Whisper wills a door closed, it must be Forced Open or battered down.

traits.name: Concentrate
traits.text: An action with this trait requires a degree of mental concentration and discipline.

ability.name: Unwilling Teleportation
ability.text: When Lady's Whisper hits and damages a creature with its claw Strike, it can choose to teleport that creature to any open space within 15 feet, even if Lady's Whisper can't see the destination. The target can attempt a DC 30 Will saving throw to resist this effect.

traits.name: Conjuration
traits.text: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the conjuration school of magic, typically involving summoning, creation, teleportation, or moving things from place to place.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text: This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.

traits.name: Teleportation
traits.text: Teleportation effects allow you to instantaneously move from one point in space to another. Teleportation does not usually trigger reactions based on movement.

universal_monster_ability.name: Darkvision
universal_monster_ability.text: A monster with darkvision can see perfectly well in areas of darkness and dim light, though such vision is in black and white only. Some forms of magical darkness, such as a 4th-level *darkness* spell, block normal darkvision. A monster with greater darkvision, however, can see through even these forms of magical darkness.

link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 49
link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 49
